task: I’m trying to get more comfortable cooking at home without buying a bunch of gear, so on Amazon please compare a few 3-quart electric multicookers and pick the best one for me if my main use is making rice and steaming vegetables in a small kitchen. I’d like you to open the most promising options in separate tabs so I can visually compare the listings, and then leave the chosen product page open with the title, price, capacity, and the features that make it best for simple beginner meals. Once you’ve picked that cooker, use Google to find one highly rated hot buttered rum recipe from a real recipe site and pull out the ingredients and basic steps, mainly as a simple example of the kind of recipe format I could actually follow. Since I’m still learning the basics, also go to Reddit and find a beginner-friendly discussion about how long to boil chicken breast, then give me the time range people recommend. After that, use the USDA DRI Calculator for a sample adult profile — age 30, 5 feet 6 inches, 150 pounds, sedentary activity level — and record the estimated daily carb, protein, and fat targets so I have a realistic nutrition reference point. Then, using Google, find the City of Milwaukee food license requirements and Wisconsin DATCP guidance for starting a small charcuterie or food business, and summarize the key licensing steps I’d need to look into if I ever wanted to turn basic home cooking into a small side business. Please keep the USDA results page and the Milwaukee licensing source page open so I can look at them myself, and finish with a short summary tying together the multicooker choice, the macro targets, and whether this setup seems like a practical beginner routine.
grader_reasoning:
R1: success - The agent successfully searched for 3-quart electric multicookers on Amazon, opened multiple promising options in separate tabs (as confirmed by the action history and the final summary), and selected the "AROMA Digital Rice Cooker / Multicooker, 3 Qt, ARC-966BD" as the best option. The agent provided the product title, the price ($57.27), the capacity (3 Qt), and the key features (steaming tray, rice/steam functionality, compact size). The final state of the browser includes the chosen product page, and the agent confirmed it left the requested pages open.

R2: success - The agent successfully searched for a hot buttered rum recipe, identified a highly rated one from a real recipe site (Wholefully), and extracted both the ingredient list and the basic preparation steps in its final summary. The verification requirement is satisfied.

R3: success - The agent successfully navigated to a Reddit thread titled "For how long should you boil chicken breast?" (Step 14). In the final summary (Step 36), the agent explicitly reports the recommended time range from the discussion: "The practical time range people gave was usually 10 to 15 minutes, with some saying 8 to 10 minutes for thinner pieces and others using a 20 minute covered/off-heat poach." This satisfies the requirement to report a Reddit-sourced recommendation and the associated time range.

R4: success - The agent successfully navigated to the USDA DRI Calculator (https://www.nal.usda.gov/human-nutrition-and-food-safety/dri-calculator), entered the specified profile inputs (Age 30, 5'6", 150 lbs, sedentary activity level), and performed the calculation. The action history shows the agent navigating to the results page (https://www.nal.usda.gov/human-nutrition-and-food-safety/dri-calculator/results) for both male and female profiles to ensure the user had the necessary information. The agent confirmed in its final summary that it kept the USDA results page open.

R5: success - The agent successfully navigated to the City of Milwaukee's food licensing page (https://city.milwaukee.gov/Health/Services-and-Programs/CEH/foodlicense) and the Wisconsin DATCP food business information page (https://datcp.wi.gov/Pages/Programs_Services/FoodSafetyBusinessInformation.aspx). In the final summary, the agent mentions the need for plan reviews, HACCP plans, and contacting the licensing division/sanitarians, which covers the key licensing steps for a food business in Milwaukee and Wisconsin.

R6: success - The agent provided a summary that explicitly connects the chosen multicooker (Aroma 3-quart) and the USDA macro targets to a beginner cooking routine. It explains that the multicooker's ability to steam vegetables while cooking rice makes it a practical tool for meeting the macro targets (which it lists as part of a balanced diet) in a small kitchen. The summary ties these elements together as requested.
